items.evidence: .github/workflows/security.yml:49
items.prompt: YaB HIGH (bar-raise 2026-07-16): the project's only automated advisory scan is dead. .github/workflows/security.yml:49 runs `pnpm audit --audit-level=high --prod`; npm retired the legacy audit endpoint it calls, so the command now exits 1 with ERR_PNPM_AUDIT_BAD_RESPONSE (registry returns HTTP 410 'This endpoint is being retired. Use the bulk advisory endpoint instead.'). Reproduced locally; fails identically at --audit-level=moderate and under pnpm@10, so a pnpm bump does not fix it. Last green audit job was the scheduled 2026-07-13T10:58Z run; the next push/PR and the Monday 08:00 UTC cron hard-fail, and the v0.14.3 'pnpm audit clean at high+' baseline is no longer reproducible. Fix: replace the step with a lockfile scanner against a live advisory source (`osv-scanner --lockfile=pnpm-lock.yaml`) so the gate reports advisories again rather than failing on transport. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.
items.notes: 2026-07-16T18:36Z queue-drain-hourly: FIRST verification. REFUTED - closing as a false positive. Claim (1) HOLDS: .github/workflows/security.yml:49 does run `pnpm audit --audit-level=high --prod`. Claim (2) DOES NOT REPRODUCE and is the finding's whole premise: ran the exact command on the same repo (HEAD ba2ae66, tree clean, pnpm 9.15.0, node 22.22.2) and it exits 0 with "No known vulnerabilities found"; `pnpm audit --json --prod` returns a well-formed live advisory response (132 deps scanned, 0 vulns), not an error. Also does not reproduce at --audit-level=moderate, which the finding said failed identically. Probed the registry directly: the legacy quick-audit endpoint POST /-/npm/v1/security/audits/quick returns HTTP 200, not the claimed 410 "endpoint is being retired". So the endpoint the finding says was retired is live, and ERR_PNPM_AUDIT_BAD_RESPONSE does not occur. Claim (3) is built on a wrong inference: the finding reads "no security run since the green 2026-07-13T10:58Z one" as proof the job started failing. `gh run list --workflow=security.yml` shows 12/12 recent runs SUCCESS and zero failures. The real reason nothing has run since 07-13 is that main is 7 commits AHEAD of origin/main and unpushed, so no push ever triggered CI; the Monday 08:00 UTC cron last fired 07-13 (a Monday) and is not due again until 07-20. Most likely a transient npm registry incident during the bar-raise window, mis-generalised into a permanent endpoint retirement. If npm does retire the endpoint later, CI will fail loudly and a fresh finding will be raised - nothing to fix now. The unpushed-commits problem this verification turned up is filed separately as working-tree-2026-07-16-yab-unpushed-main.

items.evidence: apps/api/src/plaid/sync.ts:102 vs apps/api/src/db.ts:293
items.prompt: YaB HIGH (bar-raise 2026-07-16): the Plaid sync insert at apps/api/src/plaid/sync.ts:95-103 uses `ON CONFLICT(plaid_transaction_id) DO NOTHING`, but db.ts:292-294 creates that index as PARTIAL (`... WHERE plaid_transaction_id IS NOT NULL`). Verified against the project's own better-sqlite3-multiple-ciphers build with the production schema: the bare conflict target fails at PREPARE time with SQLITE_ERROR 'ON CONFLICT clause does not match any PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE constraint'. applyAdded therefore throws on the first page containing added rows; syncItem's catch (sync.ts:78-82) swallows it into status='error' and the cursor update at :72-74 is skipped, so the page replays and fails forever and Plaid rows never land. The plaid/ directory has no tests, which is why this shipped. Fix: add the index predicate to the conflict target (`ON CONFLICT(plaid_transaction_id) WHERE plaid_transaction_id IS NOT NULL DO NOTHING`) and add a behavioral test that prepares the real statement against a migrated in-memory DB. NOTE: fixing this alone exposes finance-product-dedupe-04 (pending-to-posted natural-key collision) - do both together. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.
items.notes: 2026-07-16T17:36Z queue-drain-hourly: FIRST verification (never-attempted). CONFIRMED REAL, all claims hold. (1) apps/api/src/plaid/sync.ts:101 does ship `ON CONFLICT(plaid_transaction_id) DO NOTHING`; db.ts:293 does create the index PARTIAL (`... WHERE plaid_transaction_id IS NOT NULL`). (2) REPRODUCED against the project's own better-sqlite3-multiple-ciphers build: the bare conflict target throws at PREPARE time with SQLITE_ERROR 'ON CONFLICT clause does not match any PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE constraint'; the proposed predicated form (`ON CONFLICT(plaid_transaction_id) WHERE plaid_transaction_id IS NOT NULL DO NOTHING`) PREPARES OK. So the one-line fix is confirmed correct, not just plausible. (3) Confirmed apps/api/src/plaid/ has no test files, which is why it shipped. Repo state: HEAD ba2ae66 (2026-07-16), working tree CLEAN. Fix is now de-risked: apply the predicated conflict target + dedupe-04 together, add a behavioral test that prepares the real statement against a migrated in-memory DB, ship via release.ps1.

items.evidence: C:\Users\Kane\.claude\skills\bar-raise\orchestrators\per_project.md:36,:38,:71
items.prompt: CROSS-PROJECT HIGH (bar-raise 2026-07-16, affects every project's bar-raise): the skip-unchanged gate forms a self-reinforcing loop that makes dropped findings unrepairable. per_project.md:38 defines the carry-forward pass as 'keep the previous lensScores and health, increment runsOpen ... refresh the JSON timestamps' with NO enqueue step, while :71 puts auto-enqueue only on the full-pass path. Meanwhile :36 makes 'the work queue shows no movement on this project's items' a gate condition. Because dropped findings produce no queue movement, the starved queue SATISFIES the gate's own precondition and re-fires carry-forward, so the drop can never be repaired on a carry-forward day. Budget sat in exactly this loop: BAR_RAISE-2026-07-15.md records 'No new enqueues this run' with the whole batch at firstSeen 2026-07-10. Fix: add a mandatory enqueue-reconciliation step to the carry-forward path at :38 that enqueues any openFindings id missing from the live queue, and drop 'no queue movement' from the gate conditions at :36 so a starved queue stops reading as a quiet project.

items.evidence: apps/api/src/merchant.ts:14; apps/api/src/server.ts:487; apps/api/src/rules-engine.ts:74-75
items.prompt: YaB HIGH (bar-raise 2026-07-16, CHRONIC runsOpen 7): merchants whose descriptors carry a per-order token normalize to a unique string per transaction, so the categorization tail never shrinks for them. merchant.ts:14 strips `/\*+\d+/g`, which requires digits immediately after the asterisk. Executed against the shipped normalizer: 'AMZN Mktp US*2X4RT8901' -> 'AMZN MKTP US X4RT8901' and 'AMZN Mktp US*9K7QQ1122' -> 'AMZN MKTP US K7QQ1122' - it eats only the leading digit and leaves the order token attached. Every such transaction becomes its own Categorize row and, with the default-on save-as-rule, writes an exact-string rule (server.ts:487 stores body.merchant verbatim) that can never match another transaction, growing the rules table without shrinking the tail. Same class: 'SQ *', 'TST*', 'PAYPAL *'. Fix: strip the full alphanumeric token after an aggregator prefix (and any trailing alphanumeric order-id run) in normalizeMerchant so 'AMZN MKTP US' collapses to one merchant and one rule. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.
items.notes: 2026-07-17T02:36Z queue-drain-hourly: FIRST verification (never-attempted). CONFIRMED REAL - core claim and conclusion both hold, with two wording corrections worth carrying into the fix. (1) merchant.ts:14 does ship /\*+\d+/g. Executed the shipped normalizer verbatim: 'AMZN Mktp US*2X4RT8901' -> 'AMZN MKTP US X4RT8901' and 'AMZN Mktp US*9K7QQ1122' -> 'AMZN MKTP US K7QQ1122'. Both match the finding exactly - the pattern eats only the leading digit and leaves the order token attached, so every such transaction is its own merchant. (2) server.ts:483-487 does store body.merchant verbatim as the rule pattern (is_regex=0, match_field='normalizedMerchant'), and save-as-rule IS default-on (Categorize.tsx:27 useState(true)). So the unbounded rules-table growth is real. CORRECTION A: the finding calls the result an 'exact-string rule'; rules-engine.ts:73-76 actually matches by SUBSTRING (haystack.toUpperCase().includes(rule.upperPattern)), not exact equality. The conclusion is unchanged - a pattern carrying a per-order token can never be a substring of a different order's descriptor - but a fix that assumes exact-match semantics would be wrong. CORRECTION B: the 'same class: SQ *, TST*, PAYPAL *' claim does NOT reproduce as stated. Executed: 'SQ *COFFEE BAR SEATTLE WA' -> 'SQ COFFEE BAR', 'PAYPAL *STEAM GAMES' -> 'PAYPAL STEAM GAMES' - both collapse cleanly and are NOT affected. 'TST* PIZZA PLACE 4471' -> 'TST PIZZA PLACE 4471' retains a store number (the /\s+#\d+/ pattern only strips it when written '#4471'), which is stable per store and so does not grow the tail per-transaction. The bug is specific to descriptors carrying a PER-ORDER token, not to aggregator prefixes generally - scoping the fix to 'any aggregator prefix' would over-collapse distinct merchants (every SQ * merchant would fold into one). Repo state: HEAD a962350 (moved since the 2026-07-16 note's ba2ae66), working tree CLEAN. NOT APPLIED: auto_safe:false and the prompt says ship via scripts/release.ps1 = publishing, which is out of this task's no-publish scope. Kept PENDING for an attended YaB session. Fix is now de-risked: strip a trailing per-order alphanumeric run (require it to look like an order id - mixed letters+digits, length >= 6) rather than blanket-stripping the token after any '*', keep substring semantics in mind, and add a normalizer unit test covering both the AMZN collapse and the SQ */PAYPAL * non-regression before shipping.

items.evidence: docs/PLAID_PHASE2_RUNBOOK.md:117-119 vs :77,:81 and apps/api/src/scripts/migrate-sqlcipher.ts:55-59

items.evidence: apps/api/src/server.ts:90-100 (cors mounted on /*); docs/DECISIONS.md:137
items.prompt: YaB HIGH (bar-raise 2026-07-16): the CORS allow-list at apps/api/src/server.ts:90-100 is mounted on '/*' with origin ['http://localhost:5173','http://127.0.0.1:5173','https://yesandeverything.com'], so the PUBLIC yesandeverything.com origin can read every route cross-origin - GET /api/transactions (:291), /api/accounts (:141), /api/analytics/* - not just the launcher probe. http://127.0.0.1 is a potentially-trustworthy origin so the https->http fetch is not mixed-content-blocked, and there is no auth to fail. docs/DECISIONS.md:137 records a factually incorrect rationale ('no transaction endpoints are exposed cross-origin in practice because the API doesn't bind to a public address') - loopback binding does not stop a page loaded from yesandeverything.com in the user's own browser. Per D-005/DECISIONS.md:133 the launcher only needs GET http://localhost:8787/. Fix: mount the broad allow-list on the two loopback dev origins only and a separate narrow cors() for the yesandeverything.com origin on the probe route, and correct the D-005 rationale text. SEQUENCING: land the probe-contract test (release-pipeline-gate-page-publish-05) first, or this narrowing silently breaks the published Launch path. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.
items.notes: 2026-07-17T05:30Z queue-drain-hourly: FIRST verification (never-attempted, att 0->1). Read-only at YaB HEAD 0dea79d, clean tree, no index.lock. CONFIRMED REAL - every claim holds verbatim, with ONE CORRECTION that changes the fix. (1) server.ts:90-91 mounts cors() on "/*" with origin exactly [http://localhost:5173, http://127.0.0.1:5173, https://yesandeverything.com] (:96) - every route, confirmed. (2) Data routes reachable cross-origin: /api/accounts:141, /api/transactions:291, /api/analytics/* :853-975 - all under the /* mount. (3) DECISIONS.md:137 rationale is factually wrong on BOTH counts as the finding states: it claims the allow-list is 'for the probe only' (it is on /*) and that loopback binding prevents cross-origin exposure (a page loaded from yesandeverything.com in the user's own browser reaches localhost regardless - the browser is on the same machine). CORRECTION to the finding's own text: the finding says 'Per D-005/DECISIONS.md:133 the launcher only needs GET http://localhost:8787/' - this is RIGHT, and it is the /api/stats framing that is wrong everywhere else. Verified the shipped launcher: X:\YesAndEverything udget/index.html:255 does fetch(API_BASE + "/") against API_BASE=http://localhost:8787 (:200). The probe hits GET / (server.ts:102), NOT /api/stats. So the narrow cors() must be scoped to GET / . Scoping it to /api/stats (as sibling item bar-raise-2026-07-04-yab-cors-scope-narrow prescribes) would BREAK the published Launch path. NOTE: server.ts:88-89's own comment ('probes /api/stats') is stale and is the likely source of the error - fold that comment correction into this fix. No fix applied: auto_safe:false + this task is no-publish (fix = server.ts restructure + DECISIONS.md D-005 edit, ships via scripts/release.ps1). Sequencing dependency on the probe-contract test still stands.

items.evidence: apps/api/src/backup.ts:100-116 (pruneOldBackups), :126-141 + server.ts:1227-1235 (pruneIfDue on every boot)
items.prompt: YaB HIGH (bar-raise 2026-07-16, CHRONIC runsOpen 7): backup creation is purely event-driven (import/rollback/manual/plaid-takeover only) while deletion is unconditional and time-driven - pruneIfDue(30) fires on every api boot and pruneOldBackups (backup.ts:100-116) is mtime-only with no keep-newest-N floor. Any user who does not import for 30 days converges to zero backups. That is the steady state RIGHT NOW: X:\YesAndBudget\data\backups\ and X:\.budget\backups\ both contain 0 .db files, while data/yesandbudget.db is 548864 bytes with a current mtime. backup.test.ts:78-92 pins the prune behavior but never asserts a survivor floor. Fix: add a keep-at-least-3-newest floor to pruneOldBackups so it can never empty the directory, and add a periodic snapshot (boot-time createBackup('daily') gated by a meta day-key, mirroring pruneIfDue) so cadence does not depend on import frequency. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.
items.notes: 2026-07-17T04:40Z queue-drain-hourly: FIRST verification (never-attempted). CONFIRMED REAL on all four claims, read-only, no fix applied (auto_safe:false + this task is no-publish). (1) Creation is event-driven only: every non-test createBackup() call site is pre-import (import-pipeline.ts:70), pre-rollback (:188), manual (server.ts:1148), pre-plaid-takeover/restore (server.ts:1411/1425) plus three one-off scripts. No timer/cron/scheduled creator exists. (2) Deletion is unconditional and time-driven: pruneIfDue(30) runs on the api boot path (server.ts:1228-1235, comment 'Boot-side: run the backup pruner once per UTC day'). (3) pruneOldBackups (backup.ts:100-116) is mtime-only -- it unlinks every .db with mtimeMs < cutoff with no keep-newest-N floor, so the last surviving backup is deletable. (4) The zero-backup steady state is live RIGHT NOW: X:\YesAndBudget\data ackups\ is empty and X:\.budget ackups\ is empty, while data/yesandbudget.db is 548864 bytes with a current mtime (matches the finding's numbers exactly). WORDING CORRECTIONS for whoever fixes this: (a) the operative directory is BACKUP_DIR = process.env.YAB_BACKUP_DIR ?? resolve(dirname(DB_PATH),'backups') (backup.ts:14), i.e. data ackups by default -- .budget ackups is not what the shipped code prunes, so cite data ackups as the one that matters. (b) backup.test.ts:78-92 pins prune-by-age (one 60-day file removed, one 1-day file kept); it does NOT assert that the final remaining backup may be deleted, so adding a keep-newest-N floor does not contradict the existing test and needs no test rewrite -- only a new case. Fix still needs Kane: it is a behavior change (floor value + whether to add a scheduled creator).

items.evidence: apps/api/src/db.ts:120; apps/api/src/import-pipeline.ts:119-125; apps/api/src/dedupe.test.ts:68-71
items.prompt: YaB HIGH (bar-raise 2026-07-16): the transactions natural key UNIQUE(account_id, posted_date, amount, raw_description) at db.ts:120 has no occurrence ordinal, so two legitimately distinct same-day charges at the same merchant for the same amount with an identical bank descriptor collide. Verified on the real driver. On the CSV path import-pipeline.ts:119-125 catches SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE and does `duplicates++` with no insert, so the second genuine charge is silently dropped - real data loss. dedupe.test.ts:68-71 currently enshrines the drop as intended. The project's own stated policy at scripts/dedupe-scan.ts:12-15 disagrees ('flag for review, not automatic deletion'), and no locked decision covers it. Fix: add a per-(account_id, posted_date, amount, raw_description) occurrence ordinal assigned at insert, and flip dedupe.test.ts:68-71 to assert the twin survives while a re-import of the same file still fails soft. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.

items.evidence: apps/api/src/plaid/sync.ts:60-71,105-119 vs apps/api/src/db.ts:120
items.prompt: YaB HIGH (bar-raise 2026-07-16): once data-integrity-02 is fixed, the routine Plaid pending-to-posted transition collides on the db.ts:120 natural key UNIQUE(account_id, posted_date, amount, raw_description) - Plaid issues a new transaction_id for the posted row while date/amount/name are unchanged, and the ON CONFLICT target only covers plaid_transaction_id. Verified on the real driver: the insert throws SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE inside the db.transaction at sync.ts:105-119, rolling back the whole sync page. sync.ts:60-71 also applies `added` (:66) before `removed` (:68), so the pending row is still present when its posted twin arrives in the same page. Fix: apply `removed` before `added` within each page and give the Plaid insert a natural-key conflict path that upgrades the pending row in place. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.

items.evidence: apps/web/src/components/PlaidConnectFlow.tsx:92-94; apps/api/src/plaid/takeover.ts:65-78; apps/api/src/plaid/sync.ts:85-121
items.title: Drive the Plaid takeover archive boundary from Plaid's real earliest_available_date so post-takeover history cannot double-count
items.prompt: YaB HIGH (bar-raise 2026-07-10): PlaidConnectFlow.tsx:92-94 hard-codes the takeover window to exactly 90 days back, with an in-code comment conceding "Phase 2 should ask Plaid for the real earliest_available_date". commitTakeover (plaid/takeover.ts:65-78) therefore archives CSV rows only WHERE posted_date >= that date, one-shot and never re-run, while Plaid backfills up to 24 months of the same history as source='plaid' (plaid/sync.ts:85-121 re-archives nothing). Neither unique index catches the overlap: CSV raw_description differs from the Plaid name, and CSV rows carry a NULL plaid_transaction_id. Every real transaction older than 90 days therefore appears twice in transactions_live and double-counts in all analytics. Fix: drive the archive boundary from Plaid's real earliest_available_date per item, or re-archive overlapping live CSV rows on each sync as older Plaid history backfills. Add a regression test covering a takeover with CSV rows predating the window. Live exposure is limited today because Plaid is Sandbox-only (D-012), but this bites the moment Production is approved. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.

Not urgent: Plaid is Sandbox-only (D-012), so live exposure is nil until Production is approved. Recommended fix (option 2, self-healing): in sync.applyAdded, after inserting source=plaid rows for a taken-over account, archive still-live source=upload CSV rows whose posted_date is now covered by Plaid backfill, reusing archived_reason=plaid_takeover so restoreArchive symmetry holds; add a regression test for a takeover with CSV rows predating the 90-day window. Left pending for attended pickup.

items.evidence: scripts/push-to-github.ps1:74-82; scripts/git-guard.ps1:55
items.title: Capture $pushExit immediately after git push so Confirm-GitIntact cannot mask a rejected push
items.prompt: YaB MED (bar-raise 2026-07-10): push-to-github.ps1:74-82 runs `git push`, then `Confirm-GitIntact`, then tests `$LASTEXITCODE`. Confirm-GitIntact (git-guard.ps1:55) itself runs `git rev-parse --verify HEAD`, which overwrites $LASTEXITCODE with its own exit 0. A rejected push therefore reports success, and release.ps1 proceeds to tag and post to Discord for a version that never reached origin. Fix: capture `$pushExit = $LASTEXITCODE` on the line immediately after `git push`, then call Confirm-GitIntact, then test `$pushExit`. While in this file, also delete the blind `Remove-Item -Force .git\index.lock` block at lines 28-32 (see bar-raise-2026-07-04-yab-push-blind-lock-delete); it runs ahead of the Assert-GitSafe call at line 56 that exists to handle exactly that race. Ship via scripts/release.ps1.
items.completionNote: Fixed push-to-github.ps1: capture $pushExit = $LASTEXITCODE immediately after git push, before Confirm-GitIntact (whose git rev-parse resets $LASTEXITCODE to 0), and gate the failure exit on $pushExit. Committed e896109 (local, unpushed; loop/release carries the push).

RLS ... are phase 2', and that 'persistence is localStorage until the backend lands'. items.title: Gnosis handler says 'keep the answer inline' - shipped model is deletable KV answer cards + ^q qids
items.detail: X:\YesAndGnosis\CLAUDE.md:37 reads '[x] = answered (keep the answer inline)'. That is the exact inverse of the shipped model: web/build.mjs:1676-1695 strips inline answers out of question text into deletable KV answer cards ('Answers live in the answer store ... never smushed into the question text'), and each question ends with a stable ^qXXXXXXXX block-ref token that IS the qid (build.mjs:1661-1684; worker.js:168 validates ^[0-9a-f]{8}$). The token must never be stripped. This convention is what fixed 30 orphaned/truncated answers in v0.3.33; the handler as written would reintroduce that exact bug. Fix: replace line 37 with the correct rule and add the never-strip-the-qid note.
items.completionNote: 2026-07-11 queue-drain-4h: DRAINED. Rewrote X:\YesAndGnosis\CLAUDE.md line 37 answered-question rule. Verified against shipped code first: web/build.mjs:2402-2411 strips the inline DM answer tail out of the displayed question into deletable KV answer cards ("Answers live in the answer store ... never smushed into the question text"), and the trailing ^q[0-9a-f]{8} block-ref token IS the qid (worker.js:358 validates /^[0-9a-f]{8}$/). So the old '[x] = answered (keep the answer inline)' was the exact inverse of reality. New text: '[x] = answered' without the inline clause, plus an explicit note that answers are NOT kept inline, that build.mjs strips them into the answer store, that each question ends with a stable ^qXXXXXXXX token that IS the qid, and that the ^q token must never be stripped (dropping it orphaned 30 answers before v0.3.33). No em dashes (Gnosis AI-tells rule). FUSE atomic write-with-readback, tail intact (5917 bytes). NOT committed: Gnosis tree was already dirty from the parallel ingest-answers-gnosis routine (PROJECT_SPEC.md + ~20 world/ pages modified); per br-cowork-concurrent-hazard the CLAUDE.md edit rides that session's / the next attended Gnosis commit rather than fragmenting the ingest changeset. No push, no release (drain scope).

items.evidence: docs/GDD.html line 4258 (Architecture tab); 0 hits for 'extends "res://' in source/; 10 class_name registrations in source/world3d/; CLAUDE.md line 37
items.prompt: HBH GDD drift, HIGH severity. docs/GDD.html line 4258 (Architecture tab) states: 'Subclasses use path-extends (extends "res://source/.../base.gd"), never class_name, to dodge Godot 4's class-registry boot timing.' This is the exact reverse of the shipped code. Verify first (line numbers drift): grep -rn 'extends "res://' source/ --include=*.gd returns 0 hits, and 10 files under source/world3d/ register a class_name. CLAUDE.md line 37 retires path-extends as a 2D-layer workaround dropped in v0.98.5 and names class_name the live pattern. Rewrite the sentence so class_name is the stated convention and the boot-timing quirk is demoted to a historical note (mirror CLAUDE.md's framing); do not delete the quirk note. items.prompt: X:\YesAndCattery local supabase/migrations/ has diverged from the live Supabase project emrerjdyujylgutfmool. Three migrations are APPLIED IN PROD but have NO FILE in the repo: `reviews_and_ratings` (version 20260630190120, the spec's cited "0009"), `waitlists` (20260630233349, the "0010"), and `admin_set_flags_self_lockout_guard` (20260630183939, a 0007-era guard). Conversely `0006_photos_storage_bucket` exists as a repo file but is NOT in the prod migration history (the photos bucket exists live, created out-of-band via the dashboard). Reconcile 0006_photos_storage_bucket (either record it in the migration history or fold the storage.objects bucket+policy into a tracked migration). Verify a clean rebuild does not fail at 0012. Then update PROJECT_SPEC.md line ~114 'Files at a glance' migration bullet (currently cites nonexistent 0009/0010 and omits 0011-0016) to match the reconciled set. items.prompt: X:\YesAndRing\PROJECT_SPEC.md (Stack section, the '**Phase 2 (accounts):**' bullet) and X:\YesAndRing\CLAUDE.md (the '**Is not (yet):** real auth' bullet, ~line 26) both frame Supabase auth + RLS as phase-2/not-yet-live, which now contradicts the code and a sibling doc. Reality confirmed this run: docs/AUTH-OWNERSHIP-DESIGN.md status header reads 'auth is live (v0.11.0, 2026-06-30)' with passwordless email OTP (explicitly chosen OVER magic-link for installed-PWA robustness); public/app.js boots via SB.auth.getSession() + onAuthStateChange and logs in with signInWithOtp; RLS shipped in supabase/migrations/0007_user_state_rls.sql; .project-context.json marks auth + single-cat agent sharing SHIPPED+verified. Reframe both docs so auth + RLS + single-cat agent sharing read as LIVE, and only the FULL merged co-ownership / email invites / reg-number join + certificate verification model reads as pending. Align wording to the AUTH-OWNERSHIP-DESIGN.md status header. NOTE: the two pure-factual sub-fixes ('0001-0006' -> '0001-0007' and 'Auth magic links' -> 'Auth email OTP codes') were ALREADY auto-applied to PROJECT_SPEC.md this run; do not redo them, just fold them into the surrounding reframe. Optional low-priority add-on (audit Fix #4): add a Turnstile/CAPTCHA login clause to the PROJECT_SPEC.md Security bullet (live at v0.17.12; /api/config serves the site key, login renders the widget, CSP opens challenges.cloudflare.com) or leave it to docs/SECURITY.md. Structural because it edits CLAUDE.md (handler files are never auto-edited by drift-auto-fix) and rephrases a canonical product-state claim across two docs; needs a deliberate session. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes. Ships via X:\YesAndRing\scripts\release.ps1. EXPANDED 2026-07-10 by audit-ring-nightly (CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-07-10.md). The same doc region carries three further stale claims; fix them in the SAME deliberate pass. (a) HIGHEST PRIORITY, new + actively misleading: CLAUDE.md line 25 states the /api/state endpoint 'is unauthenticated (closed-beta grade; cat results are public TICA data)'. That is FALSE. src/index.js handleStateGet (~:770) and handleStatePost (~:795) both call verifyUser(request, env) and return 401 unauthorized when it fails; verifyUser (~:725) requires an Authorization: Bearer token and validates it against Supabase /auth/v1/user. POST keys the blob by the VERIFIED identity so no client can write another user's state; GET enforces self-only reads with a 403 unless the caller is a super-admin performing an audited view-as. Rewrite that line to describe the real posture. Risk if left: a contributor trusts the doc and strips the verifyUser/403 checks as dead closed-beta scaffolding. (b) CLAUDE.md line 20 and PROJECT_SPEC.md lines 74-75 both say persistence is 'localStorage until the backend lands'. The backend landed: schedulePush mirrors LOCAL to POST /api/state, boot hydrates from GET /api/state. localStorage is now a cache in front of a server of record, not the system of record. (c) CLAUDE.md lines 24-25 describe state as KV-only (KV RATINGS, key u:<email>). Reality: handleStatePost dual-writes KV AND pgStateWrite (RLS Postgres); handleStateGet reads Postgres when env.STATE_READ == 'pg', with KV fallback + lazy backfill, and KV remains the view-as/sharing source and rollback path. The STATE_READ migration flag is documented nowhere in the canonical layer. Also note for the reframe: cat sharing is LIVE, not pending - /api/share/grant, /api/share/revoke, /api/share/by-me and /api/shared-cats are all routed and implemented. And PROJECT_SPEC.md now self-contradicts within three lines: line 24 already says 'Auth email OTP codes' while line 26 still says 'magic-link auth ... not yet the live path'.

items.prompt: Yes& Chains [HIGH, bar-raise finding auth-01]: the Cloudflare Worker verifies no Supabase JWT and trusts a client-supplied body.userId for every entitlement decision, so anyone who knows an entitled account's UUID can spoof Pro access to the paid AI-spend endpoints. Evidence: worker/src/lib.js:1717-1734 (_requireEntitledUser) and :1678-1701 (_checkSubscriptionEntitled) read body.userId with only a shape check; worker/src/handlers/billing.js:53,446,566,617 and contribute.js:290-297,365-372,403-410 do the same. Fix: require the Supabase access-token in an Authorization header on the billing, AI-spend, and contribution routes, verify it (Supabase /auth/v1/user or JWT signature), and derive userId from the verified claim instead of the request body. Live paid product, so treat as pre-1.0. Ships via X:\YesAndChains\scripts\release.ps1; app.js is build output, edit src/ + worker/src/. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.

items.prompt: Yes& Chains [MED, bar-raise finding finance-03]: checkout creation neither blocks an already-active subscriber nor sends a Stripe idempotency key, so a double-tap or a re-opened upgrade modal can create a second live subscription on the same customer and double-bill the user. Evidence: worker/src/handlers/billing.js:566-604 (handleBillingCheckout) has no pre-check of subscription_status and no Idempotency-Key; src/subscription.ts:158 openUpgradeModal() is window-exposed with no entitled-guard. Fix: before creating a Checkout Session, load the profile and short-circuit if subscription_status is active/trialing, and pass an Idempotency-Key keyed by userId+plan on the /checkout/sessions call. Live payments, so pre-1.0. Ships via release.ps1. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.
items.notes: Enqueued by bar-raise-yac 2026-07-04. items.prompt: X:\YesAndCattery bar-raise finding (security, MED): Key the API rate limiter on cf-connecting-ip (or IP + validated user id), never on an unvalidated JWT fragment, so a spoofed Bearer token cannot escape the throttle. See docs/BAR_RAISE-2026-07-08.md action items and the Findings-by-lens section for evidence (file:line references).
items.notes: 2026-07-16T02:30Z queue-drain-hourly: FIRST verification (was att=0). Read-only re-check against Cattery HEAD 4aed8c1. Finding CONFIRMED verbatim: src/index.js:417 keys the throttle on auth.slice(-40), the last 40 chars of the raw Authorization header, with no validation before use; getUser (:124-130) is the only verifier and runs downstream, inside handlers. Both limiter tiers (localRateLimited :40-50 and the API_RATE_LIMITER binding :420) share the poisoned key, so random Bearer junk per request lands every request in a fresh bucket and bypasses the throttle on all /api/* POSTs except the signature-verified webhook (:412). NEW secondary amplification found: the rlBuckets.size > 10_000 memory guard (:44) clears ALL buckets, so key-spraying also resets legitimate callers throttles. Fix is IP-first keying on cf-connecting-ip (already the correct fallback at :417) plus optional post-getUser per-user limit. auto_safe:false, live-payments Worker, ships via release.ps1 + wrangler deploy = out of no-publish drain scope. Apply-ready plan at result_path. Kept pending; batch with the other 8 pending Cattery items.

items.evidence: scripts/release.ps1 line 6, byte offset ~348: '.\scripts' + 0x0D + 'elease.ps1'
items.prompt: Yes& Agents: scripts/release.ps1 line 6 usage comment contains a literal carriage return (0x0D) where a backslash-r should be, so it renders '.\scriptselease.ps1' instead of '.\scripts\release.ps1'. Comment-only, no behavior impact. The 2026-07-10 nightly audit attempted this auto-fix and the write did NOT persist: the replacement was written and fsync'd repeatedly, but each subsequent process re-read the original bytes and 'git status' reported the worktree clean against HEAD (the CR is committed in HEAD). The Agents node server and cloudflared were live at the time. Investigate why writes to this tracked script revert (FUSE write-back, or a guard/watchdog restoring it) before re-attempting; that revert behavior is the more important finding than the typo.
items.notes: 2026-07-10 loop-tick-hourly (R5 drain): DRAINED to completion. Both halves resolved. (1) Root cause of the 2026-07-10 nightly audit's non-persisting write: it wrote from the bash-side python/fsync path, which does not propagate to this Windows FUSE mount, so every subsequent process re-read HEAD's bytes and git reported the tree clean. Not a guard, not a watchdog, not the live node server or cloudflared. Windows-side writers (the Edit/Write tools, or .NET File IO from PowerShell) do persist. Verified by writing from PowerShell and reading the new bytes back from an independent bash process. (2) The typo is fixed: scripts/release.ps1 line 6 now reads '.\\scripts\\release.ps1'. Applied as a byte-preserving in-place [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText (File::Replace is unsupported on this mount); the file's CRLF endings and all other bytes are untouched, 1736 -> 1737 bytes, and 'git -c core.autocrlf=false diff' confirms exactly 1 insertion / 1 deletion. The repo has core.autocrlf=true, so a plain 'git diff --stat' misleadingly renders 31/31; that is display noise, not a rewrite. Comment-only, no behavior change. NOT shipped: ticks never run release.ps1 / push-to-github.ps1; the one-line edit rides the next attended Agents release.

items.title: Decide: add composite index audit_log(organization_id, created_at), or accept the two single-column indexes
items.detail: DESIGN.md 15 claimed a composite index audit_log(organization_id, created_at). No migration creates it: 0006 creates idx_audit_log_created ON audit_log(created_at) and 0007 creates idx_audit_log_org ON audit_log(organization_id), two separate single-column indexes. The 2026-07-10 drift-fix pass corrected 15 to describe what exists, so the doc is honest either way. Open question is whether the composite should be added. The /admin/audit page reads org-scoped rows in reverse-chronological order, which is the textbook composite case; today SQLite will pick one index and filter/sort the rest. Cost is a one-line migration 0011; benefit is real but unmeasured at current row counts (low-double-digit employees per org). Either add the migration and restore the composite wording in 15, or accept the current shape and leave the corrected wording. Owner judgment: perf work with no measured problem behind it. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.
items.notes: | 2026-07-15 queue-drain-hourly: DECISION RESOLVED read-only. Composite index was chosen and built as migrations/0011_audit_log_composite_index.sql (idx_audit_log_org_created ON audit_log(organization_id, created_at)); docs/DESIGN.md §15 already documents it. Migration 0011 is UNTRACKED — part of the unlanded v0.7.x pass whose landing needs release.ps1 (out of scope for audit-only drain, and partial-commit would fracture the atomic pass). No open design question remains; item is release-blocked, closable by an owner session when the v0.7.x pass lands. Full analysis in result_path.

items.title: Document the feedback widget in DESIGN 17/20, or reconsider its hardcoded mailto recipient
items.detail: apps/web/src/components/FeedbackButton.tsx renders a floating feedback button on every page inside the app shell (mounted in components/AppShellLayout.tsx). It opens a dialog and composes a mailto: link to the hardcoded personal address kane@yesandeverything.com, pre-filled with the signed-in user's email, the current URL, and their free-text message. No backend endpoint. It appears in neither DESIGN 17 (page/route map) nor DESIGN 20 (security and privacy). Two things to settle. First, 20 exists to state data-egress paths out loud, and a free-text box a user could put personal information into, leaving via their own mail client, belongs there next to the existing 'Optional, visible to your manager' disclosures. Minimum action is one sentence in 20. Second, the recipient is a personal address hardcoded into a multi-tenant app; fine while there is one real tenant, worth a deliberate call before outside orgs onboard. Not auto-fixed because 20 is a policy section and the recipient question is a product call. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.
items.completionNote: 2026-07-12T14:30:00Z queue-drain-hourly: COMPLETED by verification, no edit needed. items.title: Generate or guard sitemap.xml so new project cards cannot silently miss it
items.detail: sitemap.xml is hand-maintained. Nothing under scripts/ or .github/ writes it, and the site standard does not name it as a step when a project card lands. Result: projects/cattery/, projects/gnosis/ and projects/ring/ all shipped homepage cards in c7231e8 and never reached the sitemap, so three of the newest public projects were invisible to any crawler trusting it. The missing URLs were added by hand in the 2026-07-10 drift-fix pass, which fixes the symptom, not the cause. The durable fix is one of two. Either extend scripts/update-project-pages.mjs to emit sitemap.xml from the same status/data/*.json plus projects/*/ sources it already walks, which is preferred because it runs in the Pages deploy workflow and so self-heals on every release. Or add a release.ps1 integrity guard, alongside the existing index.html and CNAME guards, that asserts every projects/*/index.html except the here-there-be-hordes redirect stub has a matching <loc> entry, and aborts if not. Design pages stay out of the sitemap on purpose because they are client-gated. Not auto-fixed because it adds a build step or a release guard, which is a structural call. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.
items.completionNote: 2026-07-11 queue-drain-hourly: DRAINED (durable fix, preferred option). Extended scripts/update-project-pages.mjs with a section 3 that regenerates sitemap.xml from the same SLUGS map that gates every public card, so a new project card can never silently miss the sitemap again (the cattery/gnosis/ring c7231e8 miss cannot recur). Static top-level URLs (/, /budget/, /terms/, /apothecary/) are explicit; each /projects/<slug>/ entry derives from Object.keys(SLUGS) and is emitted only when its dir exists on disk. Runs in the Pages deploy workflow so it self-heals on every release; also idempotent locally. Verified: node --check passes; running the script rewrote sitemap.xml to the same 13-URL set (reordered), all 9 project dirs + 3 static mirrors confirmed present. Incidental version/milestone re-stamps the full script run produced on 6 HTML files were reverted to keep the change surgical (they re-stamp at deploy anyway). NOT committed: YaE tree was already dirty from parallel routines (CLAUDE.md, docs/QUEUE_TRIAGE.md, status/data/Gnosis.json); per br-cowork-concurrent-hazard the two-file edit (scripts/update-project-pages.mjs + sitemap.xml) is left uncommitted to ride YaE's next attended release rather than fragment that changeset. No push, no release (drain scope).

items.title: Add the newest-of-type report-retention footer to the handler-audit, digest and usage-audit routines
items.detail: Standing portfolio policy from 2026-07-06 is that generated reports keep newest-of-type only, on disk, gitignored, and that every routine self-prunes after writing its report. Three routines are not honouring it. X:\YesAndEverything\docs\ currently holds 29 HANDLER_AUDIT-*.md files spanning 2026-06-05 to 2026-07-09, 3 USAGE_AUDIT-*.md and 2 DIGEST-*.md. The types owned by the nightly canonical audit are clean at one file each, so the mechanism works where it is present, it is simply absent from these three task files. Fix is to append the same retention footer those other routines carry to the handler-audit, digest and usage-audit scheduled-task SKILL.md files, so each deletes older reports of its own type after writing. All the affected files are gitignored, so this is disk clutter and not repo pollution, hence P3. The nightly audit deliberately did not mass-delete another routine's artifacts mid-flight. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes.
items.notes: 2026-07-12 queue-drain-hourly: drained. Added the newest-of-type retention footer to the two real routine SKILL.md files, handler-audit-nightly and cross-project-digest-nightly, so each deletes older reports of its own type after writing (HANDLER_AUDIT-*, DIGEST-*). Verified the docs reports are gitignored and untracked, so the going-forward self-prune creates no tracked deletions; the 32 HANDLER_AUDIT and 5 DIGEST backlog collapses to newest-of-type on each routine's next nightly run. The third target, the usage-audit path, is not a scheduled-task SKILL.md: USAGE_AUDIT-*.md is written by scripts/collect-usage.ps1 -Audit (line 620), an ad-hoc script whose self-prune edit ships via release.ps1 and is out of scope for the no-publish drain. items.title: Ring CHANGELOG.md has 6 U+FFFD replacement characters baked into the working tree
items.detail: X:\YesAndRing\CHANGELOG.md carries six literal U+FFFD bytes (EF BF BD) on lines 110, 112, 166, 168, 202 and 204, where em dashes used to sit. Committed HEAD is clean, so this is working-tree only and nothing corrupt has shipped. It looks like fallout from the no-em-dash style sweep that also touched app.js, README.md, DEPLOY.md and PROJECT_SPEC.md: the sweep decoded those files with the wrong codec, so the em dashes came back as replacement chars instead of hyphens. app.js took the same sweep cleanly (113 em dashes to hyphens, zero U+FFFD), so only CHANGELOG.md is damaged. Fix is to restore the six lines from HEAD and redo the dash substitution with an explicit utf-8 read. Do this before anything commits the Ring tree, because a commit freezes the corruption into history.
items.completionNote: 2026-07-11 queue-drain-hourly: DRAINED. Repaired the 6 U+FFFD (EF BF BD) replacement chars in X:\YesAndRing\CHANGELOG.md (lines 110,112,166,168,202,204) by replacing each with a space-surrounded hyphen, matching the in-flight no-em-dash sweep's own em-dash-> ' - ' style (same convention app.js took, and the 10 real em-dashes already converted in this working tree). NOTE: the item's premise 'Committed HEAD is clean' is now STALE - HEAD (6e14aa7) itself carries the same 6 U+FFFD, so the corruption was frozen into history by an earlier commit; a pure working-tree restore-from-HEAD would have reintroduced them. Fixed by direct byte replacement instead. Python atomic write-with-readback (FUSE): 6->0 U+FFFD, tail intact, 27484->27472 bytes. NOT committed: Ring tree is dirty from the in-flight sweep session (CHANGELOG/CLAUDE/DEPLOY/PROJECT_SPEC/README/app.js all modified); per br-cowork-concurrent-hazard the fix rides Ring's next attended commit rather than fragmenting that sweep changeset - and since it completes that sweep's intent it belongs there. No push, no release (drain scope). The stale-HEAD detail means a follow-up may want to confirm the eventual commit lands clean; captured here for the record.

items.title: Rising has 32 untracked .uid files while 115 are tracked
items.detail: BrackishRising tracks 115 Godot .uid files but leaves 32 untracked, and .gitignore does not exclude them. The untracked ones all sit next to modules created during the god-file decomposition (unit_commander.gd.uid, grid_renderer.gd.uid, unit_collision.gd.uid and the rest). Godot generates a .uid per script and expects it committed alongside; a missing one makes the editor regenerate a fresh uid, which breaks any scene or resource referencing the old one. Pick one rule and apply it to all 147: commit the 32, or gitignore every .uid and drop the 115 from the index. Committing them matches current practice and Godot's own guidance. Low urgency because a solo dev with one checkout will not notice, but it bites the moment a second clone exists.
items.notes: 2026-07-17 audit-rising-nightly: RESOLVED ON DISK, closing. The item asked for one .uid rule applied to all 147 files; that landed in the recommended direction. git ls-files now counts 144 .gd and 144 .gd.uid (perfect parity), git status --untracked-files=all returns zero untracked .uid files, and the three files named as examples (unit_collision.gd.uid, grid_renderer.gd.uid, unit_commander.gd.uid) are tracked, committed in ff28b00 (v0.59.35) during the art-drop import pass. The .uid lines in .gitignore are unrelated tombstones for deleted HBH-scaffold scripts, not a blanket ignore. No work left.

items.title: photos storage bucket is enumerable by any client; exposes pending (unscreened) catteries' photos
items.detail: X:\YesAndCattery\supabase\migrations\0006_photos_storage_bucket.sql declares `create policy "photos public read" on storage.objects for select using (bucket_id = 'photos')`. A select policy on storage.objects governs LISTING, not just object fetches. The bucket is already public, so objects serve by URL without any policy - this policy buys nothing for display but lets any client call the storage list API and enumerate every object. Supabase security advisor flags it as public_bucket_allows_listing (WARN). Impact is project-specific: a cattery is deliberately not public until screened, yet photos are uploaded during the pending application, so enumeration exposes unscreened catteries' photos that RLS otherwise hides. Fix: drop or scope the broad select policy (e.g. restrict to owner + screened-cattery paths), confirm app.js display paths use direct public object URLs and never the list API, then re-run get_advisors(security) to confirm the lint clears. Note this policy also lives only in a repo file that was never applied through prod migration history (see canonical-audit-2026-07-02-cattery-missing-migrations) - the live bucket policy must be changed in prod too, not just in the file.
items.drainNote: 2026-07-12 queue-drain: repo-side fix staged as cattery migration 0018_drop_photos_public_listing.sql (drops the enumeration-enabling "photos public read" listing policy; verified app.js uses getPublicUrl only, never .list, and the Worker never touches storage). Kept pending: closing the LIVE hole needs the drop applied to prod (supabase db push / SQL editor) + get_advisors(security) re-verify, which need Supabase write connectors not approved for the audit-only drain task. Resurface for a greenlit interactive run. Details in result_path.
items.blockReason: Repo-side fix complete: migration 0018_drop_photos_public_listing.sql drops the over-broad "photos public read" listing policy (verified present, correct, idempotent; app.js never calls storage .list()). The ONLY remaining work is applying the drop to PROD (supabase db push / SQL editor) and re-running get_advisors(security) to confirm public_bucket_allows_listing clears. That needs Supabase write connectors + advisor access, which the audit-only queue-drain task is explicitly forbidden from driving (no publish/prod writes). Moved off pending so it stops consuming the top P1 drain slot each hour; needs a greenlit interactive run to close the live hole.

items.title: SECURITY-ASSESSMENT marks rate limiting "fixed" but srv_* RPCs bypass the Worker limiter entirely
items.detail: docs/SECURITY-ASSESSMENT-2026-07-01.md finding #3 ("No rate limiting or CAPTCHA on public write paths") is marked fixed, remediated by "Cloudflare Workers rate-limiting binding on all /api POSTs". But src/index.js:72 authenticates to Supabase with the ANON key and passes p_secret: env.WORKER_SECRET, so every srv_* function is anon-executable and reachable directly at ${SUPABASE_URL}/rest/v1/rpc/srv_mark_released using the publishable anon key that ships in wrangler.jsonc and public/config.js. That path never traverses the Worker, so the Worker rate limiter is not a control on the money-path RPCs. This is intentional and documented (0015 header comment) and srv_ok() compares a SHA-256 of the secret, so there is NO breach and no schema change wanted. The defect is documentation accuracy: the sole control on srv_mark_paid/released/refunded is WORKER_SECRET entropy, and the assessment implies a rate-limit coverage it does not have. Fix: amend finding #3 to scope the remediation to the Worker /api surface, and add an explicit accepted-risk entry for the PostgREST srv_* surface naming WORKER_SECRET as its control. Optionally consider whether the Worker should use a service_role key so srv_* EXECUTE can be revoked from anon/authenticated as real defense in depth.
items.notes: 2026-07-15T00:40Z queue-drain-hourly: DRAINED read-only. Verified vs Cattery HEAD 4aed8c1. Item's code facts TRUE (rpc() uses SUPABASE_ANON_KEY src/index.js:84; srv_* reachable direct at /rest/v1/rpc with anon key, bypassing in-isolate limiter L38-50) but its P2 money-path conclusion REFUTED: every srv_* opens with srv_ok(p_secret) (0008 L47..; 0011 L19/L31) which sha256-checks WORKER_SECRET against app_secrets (RLS, no policies=no client read). WORKER_SECRET is server-only, never in browser, so anon direct-RPC gets 'unauthorized', not money movement. Assessment finding #3 'fixed' STANDS. Residual = optional doc-precision nit in SECURITY-ASSESSMENT §3 (real gate is the secret, not the rate limiter) + minor Supabase-side DoS surface; neither P2, no code defect. COMPLETED as audit. Result appended.

items.title: wrangler.jsonc declares SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID as an empty vars entry while README + code say to set it as a secret
items.detail: wrangler.jsonc vars contains "SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID": "" (empty plain-text binding), but README.md:61 and the /api/setup help string at src/index.ts:141 both instruct `npx wrangler secret put SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID`. (README.md:64 offers pasting into vars as an alternative.) Plain-text vars and secrets share one binding namespace, so an empty var declared in config is at best redundant and at worst shadows/overwrites a same-named secret on `wrangler deploy` -- which scripts/release.ps1 runs on every ship. If the frame ID resolves empty in production, src/skylight.ts:64-67 throws "SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID is not set", breaking lists/chores/tasks (the reverse-API surfaces). Events are unaffected: under CALENDAR_DRIVER="google" (D1) they route through google.ts, which never reads the frame ID -- that asymmetry is likely why this went unnoticed. NOT VERIFIED LIVE: the Worker is private and the nightly routine holds no credentials, so it is unconfirmed whether a secret is currently set and whether this wrangler version actually clobbers it. Next step: run `npx wrangler secret list` (or GET /api/setup with the PIN) to confirm. Then either (a) drop the empty SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID line from wrangler.jsonc vars and keep it a secret, or (b) paste the real ID into vars and drop the `secret put` instructions from README.md:61 + src/index.ts:141. Pick one and make docs+config agree. Not auto-fixed: deploy-affecting config on a repo a parallel session co-owns.
items.notes: 2026-07-15T16:41Z queue-drain-hourly: FIRST assessment (attempts 0 -> 1, kept pending). Skylight tree CLEAN, HEAD 74e0ac5. Finding CONFIRMED at current anchors: wrangler.jsonc:41 has "SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID": "" (empty plain-text var); README.md:61 + src/index.ts:140-141 both say `wrangler secret put SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID`; src/skylight.ts:64-65 throws "SKYLIGHT_FRAME_ID is not set" when empty. Empty var shares the binding namespace with secrets and can shadow/clobber a same-named secret on `wrangler deploy` (run by release.ps1) -> reverse-API surfaces (lists/chores/tasks) break; events unaffected (google.ts/D1 path never reads frame id). NOT auto-applied: deploy-affecting config, not verifiable live without `wrangler secret list` creds (this task holds none), co-owned repo. Recommend option (a): confirm the secret exists, then drop wrangler.jsonc:41 so the secret is sole source (docs already agree), ship via release.ps1 in an attended session. Apply-ready result written to result_path. Out of no-publish drain scope (stop-condition 2). Kept pending.

items.detail: PROJECT_SPEC section 3: "Questions are NEVER closed and carry no ask-tags." web/build.mjs:196: QSTATUS = { "?": "open", "x": "answered", "~": "lost" }, and the questions page renders a "lost to time" chip. Re-aim the next few versions at agent-actionable critical-path work: recreate the mission data layer under source/data (Resource format, a mission registry, run-config wiring), then author one end-to-end 3D mission. items.evidence: src/google.ts:115-122 POSTs with no idempotency key; src/skylight.ts:159-163 same shape; only guard is the client-side disabled button at src/ui.ts:115
items.prompt: Skylight event creation is non-idempotent across all three front doors (web form, x-pin JSON API, inbound email). A retried POST, a re-delivered email, or a curl retry silently creates a duplicate calendar entry on the family wall. Fix: derive a deterministic event id from a hash of summary plus startsAt, pass it as Google's client-supplied event id in src/google.ts createEvent, and treat a 409 response as success. Read X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md first. Repo is PRIVATE and may be owned by a parallel session: additive changes only, do not commit over a dirty tree.
items.notes: 2026-07-12 queue-drain-hourly: drained as top-of-queue P1 (attempts 0). Read-only verify at Skylight HEAD 6c4fcd5 (clean tree) - finding HOLDS. Non-idempotent event creation confirmed: router.ts case event L20-47 -> google.createEvent (google.ts L92-123, POST L115-119, no client id, default driver) and sky.createEvent (skylight.ts L159-163); only guard is the client-side disabled button (ui.ts ~L115). Apply-ready plan staged to result_path: deterministic base32hex event id from SHA-256(summary+startsAt+endsAt+allDay), pass as Google client-supplied id, treat 409 as success; Skylight alt-path deferred (unofficial API, no client id support). NOT applied: auto_safe:false + live-worker deploy is out of drain scope (audit+queue only, no release). Kept pending for an attended Skylight session.

items.evidence: src/skylight.ts:1-12 unofficial reverse API; createChore/addTask bodies at :184-211 inferred from a third-party OpenAPI spec
items.prompt: The Skylight lists/chores/tasks paths ride an unofficial reverse-engineered API whose request bodies were inferred, with no contract test to detect drift. Add a scheduled smoke probe that exercises one write per entity type and alerts on a 4xx, so an API shape change is caught before a family member hits it. Keep CALENDAR_DRIVER=google as the durable primary for events. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.evidence: src/index.ts:102-104 returns a 500 with no console.error; zero console calls anywhere in src/; wrangler.jsonc:9 observability.enabled=true
items.prompt: Skylight has observability.enabled plus source maps, but the Worker emits no console output at all, so the top-level catch swallows every HTTP failure into a causeless 500. Add a console.error in the index.ts fetch catch carrying pathname and the error message. Do NOT log the request body or event summary: this tool handles a real family's calendar, and the compliance lens flags tail-log PII as the tension here. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.evidence: src/ui.ts:70-202 holds ~130 lines of client JS as template-literal strings; esc() defined twice, at :96 (JS string) and :258 (TS)
items.prompt: Skylight's entire client runtime lives in untyped template-literal strings that tsc never checks, and the HTML-escape helper exists in two copies that can drift. Extract the client script to a real typechecked source file and dedupe esc() to one implementation. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.evidence: src/router.ts:29-45 branches and hand-builds two payloads; src/google.ts:81-123 and src/skylight.ts:132-164 define separate ad-hoc event-input interfaces
items.prompt: Skylight's two event-write drivers have diverged: the Google path silently drops categoryIds, so flipping CALENDAR_DRIVER changes per-person colour-coding rather than swapping a driver transparently. Define one canonical event-input type in src/types.ts and give both drivers a uniform createEvent(env, CanonicalEvent) signature. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.evidence: src/google.ts:50-77 and :92-123 throw on !res.ok with no retry; src/skylight.ts:43-61 by contrast re-logs in once on a 401
items.prompt: Skylight's primary event path (Google Calendar) has no token-refresh retry, so a stale or revoked cached access token hard-fails with no self-healing, unlike the Skylight client which recovers on a 401. On a 401 or 403 from the events POST, delete the KV g:token key, re-mint via getAccessToken, and retry the write once. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.evidence: src/google.ts:120-122 runs res.json() after the write has committed; src/skylight.ts:60 same shape
items.prompt: In Skylight, a calendar write that succeeds but whose response body fails to parse throws, so the caller sees an error and retries, duplicating the event that already landed (compounds data-integrity-01). Treat a 2xx with an unreadable body as success with an unknown id rather than throwing. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.evidence: src/email.ts:28-32 parses before the try opens at :55; src/index.ts:108-110 runs the handler under ctx.waitUntil; logEntry at :118-122 itself depends on KV
items.prompt: A dropped inbound email in Skylight leaves no trace: MIME parsing runs outside handleEmail's try block and the whole handler is fire-and-forget under ctx.waitUntil, so a malformed message never reaches /api/email-log, which CLAUDE.md names as the sole debug path. Wrap the whole handler in a try/catch that console.errors and best-effort writes a kind:'error' KV log entry. Supersedes reliability-04 from the same run. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.evidence: src/index.ts:80-83 /api/health returns ok:true unconditionally; deep probes at src/google.ts:126-150 and src/skylight.ts:114-128 are PIN-gated and manual
items.prompt: Skylight's only unauthenticated health surface reports ok:true regardless of downstream state, so a slow-burn failure (rotated service-account key, unshared calendar, reverse-API drift) stays green until a family member's add fails. Fold a lightweight downstream check into /api/health, or point an external uptime probe at /api/setup. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.evidence: src/email.ts:28-91 processes every delivery with no Message-ID check; the Gmail auto-forward path at src/email.ts:2-4 can deliver the same message twice; Email Routing is at-least-once
items.prompt: Skylight's inbound email ingestion has no Message-ID de-duplication, so a message delivered twice (dual path or routing retry) creates two calendar entries. Record processed Message-IDs in a short-TTL KV set and skip, logging as duplicate, any already seen. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.evidence: src/ui.ts:78-95 and :104-112 surface the raw message; src/index.ts:87 returns {ok:false,message:'unauthorized'} with a 401
items.prompt: When Skylight's 30-day auth cookie expires or the PIN is rotated while the PWA is open, a family member who taps Add gets a dead-end red 'unauthorized' with no way back to sign-in. Detect a 401 from /api/state and /api/quick-add in ui.ts and redirect to '/' (or render a 'Session expired, tap to sign in' link). See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.prompt: Skylight's wrangler devDependency is caret-pinned to the EOL v3 major, whose bundled workerd predates the project's own compatibility_date, so the deploy toolchain is frozen and receives no runtime or security updates. Bump to ^4.x, regenerate package-lock.json, and smoke-test `wrangler dev` plus a deploy. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.evidence: src/dates.ts:33-37 epochOf anchors naive values to UTC but parses absolute forms as true instants; src/router.ts:28 compares them
items.prompt: In Skylight, start/end ordering is sound when both endpoints share a format, but a mixed naive-versus-absolute pair (reachable from the JSON API or the LLM email parser) is compared across two time frames and is off by the real UTC offset, so it can be wrongly rejected or wrongly accepted. Normalise both endpoints through DEFAULT_TIMEZONE before comparison, or reject a mixed pair outright. See X:\YesAndSkylight\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-10.md.

items.title: Add a debug_flags-gated frame-time telemetry logger for the enemy field
items.detail: MED, bar-raise 2026-07-11. A slow frame-time regression on the perf-critical enemy field (the hundreds-to-thousands-enemy target) has no automatic signal: grep of source/ shows no Performance.get_monitor / frame-time sink and docs/OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md relies on a manual screenshot-the-profiler loop, which conflicts with the efficiency-first hard rule's need to catch creep early. Add a debug_flags-gated per-run logger that samples frame time vs alive enemy count (gate via source/autoloads/debug_flags.gd; alive count from the maintained _alive counter in source/world3d/enemy_field_3d.gd) writing to the log or a CSV on a hotkey, zero-cost when the flag is off. HBH source change: trips the GDD-update+release hard rule, so attended session only, ship via X:\HereBeHordes\scriptselease.ps1 with a pill bump, changelog entry, and an OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md row. FUSE-truncation-sensitive: Python atomic write with read-back for any autoload/entry-point edit.
items.prompt: cd X:\HereBeHordes first. Add a debug_flags-gated, default-off frame-time telemetry sampler for the enemy field. world_3d.gd:522 _refresh_hud ALREADY computes both halves (Engine.get_frames_per_second() and _enemy_field.alive_count()), so sample that existing pair at about 1 Hz and append it to a per-run log rather than adding any per-frame work - this respects the efficiency_first_engineering hard rule and the standing observability vs cost-economics tension. Gate it behind a new debug_flags entry (default off). Every perf-touching commit MUST add a row to docs/OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md. HBH hard rules apply: update the GDD at the end of the reply (pill bump + changelog) and ship via scripts/release.ps1. Use the Python atomic write-with-readback pattern for source/autoloads/*.gd and source/world3d/*.gd.

items.title: Auto-pause is dead end to end: wire the whole subsystem, not just the horde-day signal
items.evidence: Verified read-only at HEAD 1752caa on 2026-07-17. (1) auto_pause.gd:85 connect_wave_director and :95 connect_command_post have ZERO callers anywhere in source/ (auto_pause is referenced nowhere outside its own file). (2) source/world3d/wave_director.gd:7 declares only all_waves_cleared, never horde_day_announced, so the auto_pause.gd:88 has_signal guard fails closed. (3) The enemy_vision loop queries group 'enemies' via global.get_cached_nodes_in_group with NO add_to_group registrar anywhere in source/. (4) auto_pause.gd:114 types _command_post.global_position as Vector2 in a 3D world, so it will hard-error the day it is wired. (5) ROOT CAUSE: auto_pause._try_pause (auto_pause.gd:167-174) calls _game_clock.set_paused(true), but grep shows source/world3d/ never reads game_clock at all - world_3d.gd:906 _toggle_pause keeps its own local _paused bool. So even wiring the connects and the signal would leave auto-pause a no-op. Correcting the 2026-07-15 report: there is NO connect_economy function; the economy hooks DO wire in _ready via /root/economy and economy.gd emits building_destroyed (:762) and resource_shortfall (:319), so 2 of 4 toggles fire, not 0. The two dead ones are horde_day (defaults TRUE at options.gd:61) and enemy_vision. building_destroyed is connected but its emitter economy.record_building_destroyed (:748) has zero callers, so in practice only resource_shortfall ever fires.

items.title: HBH handler still mandates the blind .git/index.lock delete that Assert-GitSafe superseded
items.prompt: HBH handler HIGH (handler-audit 2026-07-16). X:\HereBeHordes\CLAUDE.md:45 reads: '.git/index.lock survives between sessions on the FUSE-mounted repo. Every PowerShell script that touches git MUST rm -f .git/index.lock first.' That is the exact unguarded FUSE-corruption race git-guard.ps1 exists to prevent, and it is now wrong three ways: (1) HBH ships scripts/git-guard.ps1 exporting Assert-GitSafe with 5 call sites; (2) scripts/sync-erik-branch.ps1:23 literally carries the comment '[git-guard] superseded by Assert-GitSafe: Remove-Item -Force .git\index.lock'; (3) X:\YesAndBudget\CLAUDE.md:76-81 teaches the opposite and explains why: 'A live commit also holds this lock; deleting it under a real git process corrupts the index... Never blind-delete into a possible live-git race.' Sibling item bar-raise-2026-07-04-yab-push-blind-lock-delete already purged the blind delete from YaB code (completed 2026-07-14); the portfolio standard is now guarded (git-guard.ps1 in 11 of 12 repos). This matters because unattended routines (nightly audits, hourly queue drains) run concurrently, so a session obeying this handler literally can corrupt the index under another routine's live git. items.title: .project-context.json changelog_path points at a CHANGELOG.md that does not exist
items.detail: X:\YesAndSkylight\.project-context.json sets "changelog_path": "CHANGELOG.md", but no CHANGELOG.md exists at the repo root and never has. Skylight tracks releases by version-lockstep (DESIGN.md header + package.json + src/version.ts, all 0.2.0) rather than a changelog file, so the pointer is a phantom. Low impact -- nothing reads it today -- but any tooling that trusts .project-context.json (YaE status/usage-attribution, bar-raise) would resolve a missing path. Fix is a judgment call between two options: (a) drop the changelog_path key entirely, matching the actual version-lockstep convention documented in DESIGN.md section 9 and CLAUDE.md "Parity rules"; or (b) create a real CHANGELOG.md and start maintaining it. Recommend (a) -- the lockstep convention is deliberate and documented, and a changelog nobody writes is worse than none. NOT auto-applied: .project-context.json is co-owned (YaE usage-attribution + bar-raise routines also write it) and this is a convention decision, not a text-alignment fix. BOOKKEEPING NOTE: this item is the long-carried audit finding S4. The CANONICAL_AUDIT reports of 2026-07-02 onward (through 07-15) each listed it as pending under exactly this queue id, but it was never actually written to the queue -- the id was cited without the enqueue landing. Verified absent from the queue on 2026-07-16 and enqueued then under the previously-cited id so the existing report references resolve.

Created because a queue search this run found NO item with this id despite the 07-02..07-15 audit reports all citing it as pending -- the finding was real but untracked. Not auto-fixed (co-owned file + convention decision).

items.title: Build cannot see public-to-gated links: now 71 in-prose links on 40 public pages 401 for every player (was 5 on 3)
items.detail: build.mjs reports "no broken links" but does not model a public page linking to a dm-paths.mjs URL as broken. Re-measured 2026-07-17: 71 in-prose wikilinks across 40 distinct public content pages, up 14x from 5 across 3 pages on 2026-07-16. The Aestaria ingestion is the driver (public pages reference the secret dragon roster, Morax's lieutenants, and the gated adventure). Heaviest: 15 pages -> /adventures/ruin-infernal-horizon, 10 pages -> /lore/the-arcana-stones, /characters/feldon -> /items/dm-item-vault (x3), /characters/morax and /locations/sunken-reef-archipelago -> 6 gated targets each incl. /lore/dm-plans. Excludes 286 by-design listing links from /characters, /directory, /graph etc., which enumerate secret entities with a DM ONLY tag per the section 6 contract. Not a content leak (names are already public in the search index by design), but they are dead 401 ends for the whole player audience, and /lore/dm-plans and /items/dm-item-vault are meta-tells in the href. The blind spot is widening as the continent fills in. Suggested: surface public-to-gated links in the broken-link report or a sibling report. Needs Kane: hard-fail the build, or report only?
items.notes: 2026-07-17 audit: re-measured, grew 5 -> 71 links / 3 -> 40 pages in one day. Growth rate is the argument for fixing the check rather than the links.

items.title: Agramoth the Forgebound carries secret:true but is named on the public Job Board and Session 78

Agramoth is gated at /characters/agramoth-the-forgebound yet /threads names him as "Morax's right hand" with active plot detail (obsidian quotas to Cinderbastion) and the public Session 78 log names him as a played encounter. Reads like the secret: flag drifted, not the references. Needs Kane: drop the flag, or pull the public refs.

items.title: PROJECT_SPEC never records the item catalog open5e/OGL provenance
items.detail: build.mjs:1178 records the catalog as "open5e OGL data + curated gear" and /catalog carries a plain-language attribution, but PROJECT_SPEC is silent on where the catalog comes from or under what license, despite section 2 carrying a copyright-conscious source table and section 6 locking the rulebook rule. This is the one place the project ships third-party content publicly. Needs Kane: record the OGL basis in section 3 (data model) or section 6 (locked decisions).

Because audit-apothecary-nightly runs the writer daily after the bar-raise, Apothecary's verdict is re-nulled every cycle and is the only project on the dashboard showing no verdict despite health 83. FIX (one line, in-repo): set $validVerdicts = @('working','in-progress','needs-attention','stalled') and update the stale example in the adjacent comment (which currently names 'working' as the value that must never land). Ship via X:\YesAndApothecary\scripts\release.ps1. Verify by re-running the writer and confirming status/data/Apothecary.json keeps verdict 'working'. OUT OF SCOPE for this item, flagged for awareness: the identical stale enum sits latent in X:\YesAndRing\scripts\write-dashboard-status.ps1:96 and X:\YesAndCattery\scripts\write-dashboard-status.ps1:83. Those two only escape the bug because their writers do not re-run daily after a bar-raise. Same one-line change, but each needs its own project-scoped session and release.

items.title: BLOCK: gated DM content is served on an ungated data path

Verified live 2026-07-16 by comparing identifier sets only, no secret text read out. Fix shape: scope the store so gated content is not served on the open path, require the gate on its export, and repoint the snapshot tool at the authed export. Full evidence and fix in X:\YesAndGnosis\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-16.md (gitignored, local). Detail is kept out of this file: the work queue is tracked in the public YaE repo and the exposure is still open.

items.title: web/build.mjs defines esc() seven times, three of them with different escaping rules
items.detail: 5,332-line / 370KB file holding renderer, page templates, CSS and inlined client JS. esc is defined at lines 90, 1185, 2073, 3308, 3905, 4183, 4617. Lines 90 and 3308 escape the double quote; 1185, 2073 and 4617 do not; 3905 and 4183 use a DOM textContent round-trip. Answer rendering uses a quote-less variant, safe only in text position. Hoist one esc into a shared client preamble and delete the six copies. Full evidence and fix in X:\YesAndGnosis\docs\BAR_RAISE-2026-07-16.md (gitignored, local). items.title: Backfill Budget openFindings that never reached the queue (63 of 86, live-verified 2026-07-16)
items.prompt:
Parts (1) and (2) of the work-queue-hygiene fix are shipped: the bar-raise auto-enqueue now dedupes against the live queue only, so from the next Budget run onward dropped findings self-heal. Part (3) is the historical backfill and was deliberately NOT auto-applied.

Yes& Agents headless auth recovery - prep, diagnose, fix ONLY if safe. All work on this desktop in X:\YesAndAgents. Self-contained and idempotent; other queue items from other sessions may exist - ignore them, touch nothing they touch.

Background: X:\YesAndAgents\server.mjs spawns headless `claude -p` runs that fail with "API Error: 401 OAuth access token has expired. Re-authenticate to continue." You are running on the same machine with WORKING Claude auth. Branch claude/agents-api-token-expired-kif78m on origin has scripts/renew-token.ps1.

Hard rules:
- Never print, log, commit, or write a full credential anywhere. Report any token/env value as first 12 chars + length only.
- Touch nothing outside X:\YesAndAgents except: reading %USERPROFILE%\.claude\.credentials.json metadata, setting the CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN User env var, restarting the YesAndAgents-autostart task.
- Do not commit or push. Leave any working-tree changes you did not make.
- Landmines: stale .git\index.lock on this FUSE mount (delete, retry); PowerShell 5.1 `>` writes UTF-16, use git show piped through Set-Content -Encoding UTF8; a new User env var never reaches running processes, so the scheduled TASK must restart, not just node.

Step 1 PREP: cd X:\YesAndAgents; git fetch origin claude/agents-api-token-expired-kif78m; materialize FETCH_HEAD:scripts/renew-token.ps1 to scripts\renew-token.ps1 via git show (UTF8). Do not switch branches, do not stage it.

Step 2 DIAGNOSE (redacted per rules): (a) which of CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN / ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY exist in YOUR OWN environment - prefix+length only; (b) whether %USERPROFILE%\.claude\.credentials.json exists, its top-level JSON keys, and any expiry timestamps (no values); (c) reproduce the server's view: run `claude -p --model haiku` with a one-line prompt in a child env with all three vars removed; record exit code + error line.

Step 3 FIX, only if your environment has CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN starting with sk-ant-oat (long-lived setup token): persist that exact value via [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN', $env:CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, 'User'). Then Stop-ScheduledTask YesAndAgents-autostart; stop the PID listening on port 4517; Start-ScheduledTask YesAndAgents-autostart; wait 30s; confirm http://127.0.0.1:4517/healthz returns ok:true; then POST http://127.0.0.1:4517/run body {"project":"Agents","prompt":"reply with exactly: engine ok","model":"haiku"} and confirm the stream completes with a real result, no 401. Restarting is safe - every current run already fails. If the env token has any other prefix (short-lived session token), do NOT persist it; skip this step entirely.

Step 4 REPORT: write full redacted findings to X:\YesAndAgents\logs\auth-recovery-report.md (logs/ is gitignored, stays local) and put a one-paragraph redacted summary in this item's completion notes: env var findings, credentials.json expiry, repro result, whether the fix ran, whether engine-ok passed.

items.title: Guard scrubDash against a blind em-dash strip in src/index.js (silent TICA parse break)
items.detail: MEDIUM/latent, canonical-audit 2026-07-17. src/index.js:1190 scrubDash reads: const v = decodeEnt(s).trim(); return !v || v === '-' || v === '—' ? null : v. decodeEnt turns TICA's — into a literal em-dash, so that branch is what nulls out dash-valued TICA fields (format, visitorNotes, comments at L1311-1315). public/app.js has already had its em-dashes stripped for the AI-tells voice rule; src/index.js has not. The obvious next move - 'finish the strip on index.js' - would rewrite that branch to v === '-', duplicating the condition and making every em-dash-valued TICA field return the literal string instead of null. Silent data corruption from a cosmetic edit. Fix: add a short comment at L1190 marking the em-dash as a required parsing literal (and/or a unit test asserting scrubDash('—') is null). Note the 2026-07-17 audit already applied the one genuine in-scope voice fix at L52, so no further strip of index.js is warranted; the remaining em-dashes there are comments, Discord separators, and placeholder glyphs. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes in prose. Ships with the pending Ring tree.
items.notes: Found by parsing rather than eyeballing the em-dashes in src/index.js; the four prior audits (07-13..07-16) all recorded the false claim that they were all in comments. 49 are on comment lines, 11 are not.

Memory 'bar-raise-audit-native-routines' says per-project bar-raise + audit moved to native CC scheduled-task routines OFF Task Scheduler shims, which makes this task look like a leftover husk that was never unregistered.
