items.title: The same two stale trees are queued twice each and have sat pending for a week, so the queue is describing work instead of draining it
items.prompt: Found by the working-tree scan's own dedup step on 2026-07-17, and it is really two problems wearing one coat. FIRST, duplicates: the Scheduler stale tree is queued as BOTH working-tree-2026-07-11-scheduler-stale-tree AND wq-2026-07-12-002, and the Ring stale tree as BOTH working-tree-2026-07-10-ring-stale-tree AND wq-2026-07-12-003. All four are pending. Two scanners with different id conventions (working-tree-* vs wq-*) described the same trees and neither saw the other, because dedup was by body match and the bodies were worded differently. SECOND, and worse: those trees are STILL DIRTY as of 2026-07-17. Scheduler has 8 changes (5 source files under apps/api/, docs/DESIGN.md, plus untracked migrations/0011_audit_log_composite_index.sql and scripts/sync-audit-dashboard.ps1) whose HEAD commits date to 2026-07-03 and, for open-shifts.ts, 2026-05-18. Ring has 6 changes including public/app.js and src/index.js, last committed 2026-07-04. Real source edits have sat unshipped for two weeks while the queue accumulated four items about them - exactly the rotting-tracked-item shape Kane has said to refuse. DO: (1) supersede one duplicate of each pair, keeping the richer body; (2) actually drain the two survivors - read the diffs, commit or revert per file, ship via each project's release script if the change is real and verified; (3) then fix the cause - dedup future stale-tree findings by (project, kind) rather than by body text, so differently-worded reports of the same tree collapse. Ask Kane before committing Scheduler's or Ring's source diffs; two-week-old uncommitted source may be a deliberate park rather than a forgotten one, and this item's job is to force the decision, not to guess it.
items.notes: auto_safe=false: draining these means committing or reverting two weeks of real source diffs in Scheduler and Ring - a judgement call, not a mechanical fix. The dedup-by-body weakness is why this scan nearly filed a fifth and sixth duplicate; fixing the dedup key is the durable half.

items.title: Character gate's soft/hard viewing model lives only in a code comment: hidden_from is not a gate and the spec never says so
items.detail: The site-wide character gate shipped in v0.3.19x (commit 66d2aec) and introduces a SECOND, weaker tier of hiding alongside secret:. PROJECT_SPEC.md and CLAUDE.md both contain zero mentions of: character gate, identity gate, hidden_from, /api/whoami, /dm-logout, gnosis-char, or /map-aestaria. The distinction between the two tiers is recorded only in a build.mjs code comment (line 1688): hidden_from is a client-side courtesy filter whose hidden text SHIPS IN THE HTML, while secret: is a hard worker-side gate. The implementation is correct and the comment is honest; the gap is that neither fact reaches the canonical doc every session is told to read first. Not a live exposure: zero vault files currently use hidden_from and the secret: gate is intact and verified (126 entities gated, search index redacted, questions board clean). The risk is the armed trap: the first time someone puts spoiler-bearing content behind hidden_from believing it is a gate, it ships to every player. Suggested: document the two-tier model in section 3 (frontmatter contract, next to the secret: rules) and section 6 (as the locked decision it plainly is). Needs Kane's wording; the substance is already settled by the code.
items.notes: Doc gap, not a live leak. Cheap to close now, expensive after the first hidden_from use.

items.title: /map-aestaria is a DM-gated surface documented in neither section 4 nor section 6
items.detail: /map-aestaria builds (132 KB, 86 pins, painted-labels toggle) and is worker-gated along with its three art assets (/assets/aestaria-map.jpg, -hi.jpg, -labeled.jpg), following the identical pattern to /questions-dm: built by build.mjs, appended to dm-paths.mjs, gated behind the DM password. Section 4 lists player-visible surfaces so it does not belong there; section 6 documents /questions-dm with an explicit rationale for why a DM-gated surface is recorded there instead. /map-aestaria appears in neither. The precedent for placement is already set by the /questions-dm entry. Not auto-applied because section 6 is the Locked decisions section and locked is locked. Needs Kane.

items.title: Section 5 milestone stamp drifted twice in two days and went factually inverted; stop hand-maintaining the counts
items.detail: The v0.3 milestone stamp in section 5 carries hand-maintained entity/pin/location counts. It was refreshed on 2026-07-16 and was stale again within 24 hours (485 -> 574 entities). Worse, yesterday's refresh introduced a factual inversion: it read '81 pins rendered from the 86 of 108 locations that carry PUBLIC map: coordinates', but 86 is the SECRET Aestaria pin count, not the public one (actual: 167 of 181 locations carry coordinates, 81 public / 86 secret, 15 opt out with map: none). A doc describing the redaction contract asserted the opposite of the redaction reality. The 2026-07-17 pass corrected the digits and added a line pointing at build.mjs output as authoritative, but the underlying problem stands: a hand-maintained count cannot survive a project ingesting ~90 entities a day, and each refresh is a fresh chance to invert a fact. Suggested: recast the stamp as build-derived language and drop the digits entirely (e.g. 'build.mjs prints current entity, pin, and broken-link counts on every run'). Not auto-applied because dropping information from a canonical milestone is a scope call. Needs Kane.
items.notes: Digits corrected 2026-07-17; this item is the structural cure, not the patch.

Evidence: worker/src/handlers/billing.js:640-693 handles only the happy path; there is no charge.refunded or charge.dispute.created branch and no periodic sweep. Fix: add a scheduled reconciliation (Cloudflare cron) that pulls active Stripe subscriptions and repairs subscription_status in Supabase, and handle charge.refunded plus dispute events to revoke entitlement. Pairs with webhook-01 (same handler). Ships via X:\YesAndChains\scripts\release.ps1. app.js is build output, edit src/ and worker/src/. Canonical-doc writes go through tools/safe_write.py. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes. Re-verify the cited anchors before editing.

items.evidence: worker/src/handlers/billing.js:656-690 (checkout.session.completed branch; no payment_status gate, no event.id dedupe, no stripe_subscription_id persisted, 200-ACKs a missing profile)
items.prompt: Yes& Chains [HIGH, bar-raise finding webhook-01]: the Stripe webhook handler is under-hardened on a live money path. At worker/src/handlers/billing.js:656-690 the checkout.session.completed branch does not gate on payment_status == "paid" (so an unpaid or async-pending session can grant Pro), does not dedupe on event.id (Stripe retries at-least-once, so replays re-apply), does not persist stripe_subscription_id (which dataint-01 reconciliation and finance-02 dunning both need), and 200-ACKs when the profile lookup misses, silently discarding the grant with no retry. Fix all four: gate on payment_status, add an idempotency table or KV key on event.id, store stripe_subscription_id, and return a non-2xx on a missing profile so Stripe retries. Pairs with dataint-01. Ships via X:\YesAndChains\scripts\release.ps1. app.js is build output, edit src/ and worker/src/. Canonical-doc writes go through tools/safe_write.py. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes. Re-verify the cited anchors before editing.

items.evidence: worker/src/handlers/billing.js:104-218 (KV read-modify-write on the spend counters)
items.prompt: Yes& Chains [MED, bar-raise finding dataint-02]: the AI cost-cap counters are a KV read-modify-write at worker/src/handlers/billing.js:104-218, so concurrent requests interleave and the caps can overshoot. KV is eventually consistent with no compare-and-swap, so this is structural, not a tuning problem. Exposure is currently latent because CHAINS_VISIBLE is false and the AI surfaces are hidden, but it becomes a real spend risk the moment that flag flips, so land it before the flip. Fix: move the counters to an atomic primitive (a Durable Object per cap window is the natural fit, or a Supabase row with an atomic increment). Couple this with cost-01 and the CHAINS_VISIBLE flip. Ships via X:\YesAndChains\scripts\release.ps1. app.js is build output, edit src/ and worker/src/. Canonical-doc writes go through tools/safe_write.py. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes. Re-verify the cited anchors before editing.

items.evidence: worker/src/lib.js:1691 (_checkSubscriptionEntitled; no past_due branch); supabase/migrations/0018:60 (is_entitled())
items.prompt: Yes& Chains [MED, bar-raise finding finance-02]: there is no dunning decision, so a failed renewal has undefined behaviour. Neither _checkSubscriptionEntitled (worker/src/lib.js:1691) nor is_entitled() (supabase/migrations/0018:60) has a past_due branch, so a card that fails on renewal either cuts the user off instantly or keeps them entitled forever depending on what Stripe writes. Fix: decide the grace policy first (a common shape is entitled through past_due for a fixed retry window, then revoked on unpaid or canceled), record it as a Decision in DECISIONS_NEEDED.md, then implement the branch in both the worker and the migration so the two agree. This is a product decision before it is code; if the grace window is not obvious, surface the options rather than guessing. Ships via X:\YesAndChains\scripts\release.ps1. app.js is build output, edit src/ and worker/src/. Canonical-doc writes go through tools/safe_write.py. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes. Re-verify the cited anchors before editing.

items.evidence: worker/src/lib.js:1685 (entitlement check; no cache, fail-open/closed comment contradicts behaviour)
items.prompt: Yes& Chains [MED, bar-raise finding reliability-02]: every entitlement check hits Supabase live (worker/src/lib.js:1685) with no cache, so a transient Supabase blip locks out paying users, and the fail-open/closed comment at that site describes the opposite of what the code does. Fix: add a short-TTL positive-entitlement cache (60 to 300s, cache only the entitled result, never the denial, so a revocation still takes effect promptly) and correct the comment to match real behaviour. Note the tension with security: caching entitlement widens the revocation window, which is why only the positive result is cached and the TTL stays short. Ships via X:\YesAndChains\scripts\release.ps1. app.js is build output, edit src/ and worker/src/. Canonical-doc writes go through tools/safe_write.py. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes. Re-verify the cited anchors before editing.

items.evidence: worker/src/handlers/research.js:38 (handleExtractPhotosReal); research.js:131 (handleParseCourseText); neither applies DAILY_AI_SPEND_CAP_USD; photos[] length unbounded
items.prompt: Yes& Chains [MED, bar-raise finding cost-01]: two AI endpoints bypass the global spend cap. handleExtractPhotosReal (worker/src/handlers/research.js:38) and handleParseCourseText (research.js:131) never apply the DAILY_AI_SPEND_CAP_USD guard-and-accrual the other AI paths use, and photos[] has no length bound, so one request can fan out arbitrarily many vision calls. Fix: route both through the same guard-and-accrual helper the capped endpoints use, and bound photos[] to a sane maximum with a clear error past it. Couple with dataint-02 (the counter these depend on is not atomic yet) and with the CHAINS_VISIBLE flip. Ships via X:\YesAndChains\scripts\release.ps1. app.js is build output, edit src/ and worker/src/. Canonical-doc writes go through tools/safe_write.py. Solo-dev voice, no em dashes. items.evidence: scripts/discord-notify.ps1:5-7 and :16 name scripts/.discord_webhook.txt (in-repo) and the webhook names 'Apothecary Push' / 'YesAndApothecary Headline Bot'; line 39 of the same file reads Join-Path "X:\.secrets\YesAndApothecary\scripts" ".discord_webhook.txt"; CLAUDE.md:147 locks the names to 'Apothecary Release Bot' / 'Apothecary Headline Bot'
items.prompt: Yes& Apothecary [MED, bar-raise finding discord-notify-header-stale-path-and-names]: scripts/discord-notify.ps1's own header lies about the path its code reads. Lines 5-7 tell the reader the webhook files live at scripts/.discord_webhook.txt and scripts/.discord_announcements_webhook.txt inside the repo and are gitignored; line 39 actually reads them from X:\.secrets\YesAndApothecary\scripts\. Line 16's setup step names the webhooks 'Apothecary Push' and 'YesAndApothecary Headline Bot', both retired by the naming standard locked at CLAUDE.md:147 ('Apothecary Release Bot' for #apothecary-dev-log, 'Apothecary Headline Bot' for #apothecary-announcements). Consequence: anyone following the header creates a wrongly-named webhook, saves the URL where nothing reads it, and gets a silent skip at release time. Fix the header comment block only (lines 5-16); line 39 is already correct, do not touch it. Point the reader at X:\.secrets\YesAndApothecary\scripts\ and use the locked webhook names. While there: DEPLOY.md's 2026-07-17 edit removed the pointer to scripts/.discord_webhook.txt.example on the false premise that the file does not exist (it does: tracked, 23 bytes, 'PASTE_WEBHOOK_URL_HERE'), so either restore a pointer to it or delete the orphaned file. Ships via cd X:\YesAndApothecary; .\scripts\release.ps1 -Message "..." (the script owns the version bump; do not hand-edit the pill). Solo-dev voice, no em dashes. items.prompt: Yes& Agents, bar-raise 2026-07-17 HIGH (observability-02, open 8 runs). v1.1.0 wired the headless-auth alarm from probe -> /healthz -> UI banner -> watchdog toast, but the dashboard leg never refreshes: scripts/write-dashboard-status.ps1 -RefreshOnly exists (:11, :119) and NOTHING calls it at runtime, so X:\YesAndEverything\status\data\Agents.json is written only by a release. The live file currently reads heartbeatAgeMin 0.2 / tunnel.up true / auth.checkedAt 03:53Z against a heartbeat on disk of 11:40Z. The 5-minute freshness gate at :66-70 is correct code that only ever executes at release time. Consequence: if the credential dies mid-day, the desktop toasts and the phone banners but the dashboard keeps asserting auth.ok true until the next release. Fix: in scripts/start-all.ps1's while loop (20s sleep at :132), call `& scripts\write-dashboard-status.ps1 -RefreshOnly` roughly every 15th iteration (about 5 minutes), wrapped in try/catch so a failed refresh never breaks the watchdog. Do NOT call it every loop (it shells git). While there, consider the paired MED (observability-03): stamp a top-level generatedAt on the payload and derive `stale` from JSON age instead of the hardcoded `$false` at :104. Ship via `cd X:\YesAndAgents; .\scriptselease.ps1 -Message '...'`. Report: docs/BAR_RAISE-2026-07-17.md action 1.

Yes& Agents, bar-raise 2026-07-17 MED (security-02, widened from LOW by v1.1.0). GET /healthz sits above the auth gate by design (server.mjs:277) and v1.1.0 widened its body from version/uptime/pid to include the full `auth` block, whose `reason` carries up to 300 chars of the failing run's provider error text (:144). So an unauthenticated caller can read credential state and the provider's error string. Exposure is bounded (localhost default; Cloudflare Access default-deny in front of the tunnel) but the body grew the wrong way. The naive fix breaks the feature: the watchdog is itself an unauthenticated caller and needs auth.ok (start-all.ps1:84-88). Fix: key the body on the CALLER, not on a session. If the request is loopback (req.socket.remoteAddress is 127.0.0.1 / ::1 / ::ffff:127.0.0.1) AND there is no cf-ray header, return the full block as today; otherwise return only {ok:true, version}. That keeps the watchdog, the 5s UI health poll, and the auth banner whole while closing the remote read. Verify after: curl 127.0.0.1:4517/healthz still returns the auth block, and the UI banner still fires. Ship via `cd X:\YesAndAgents; .\scriptselease.ps1 -Message '...'`. Report: docs/BAR_RAISE-2026-07-17.md action 2.

items.evidence: dashboard/index.html:3047 and :3351 both fetch the same usage.json with cache:no-store plus a unique ?t= bust; :3361 re-runs on a 300000ms interval; ~38KB per load plus ~19KB/5min per tab
items.prompt: YaE MEDIUM (bar-raise-everything 2026-07-17): dashboard/index.html:3047 fetches usage.json via fetchData('usage'), and :3351 fetches the SAME https://usage.yesandeverything.com/usage.json a second time from the enrichment IIFE. Both pass {cache:'no-store'} and a unique ?t=Date.now() bust (fetchJson at :1176), and the Worker itself returns Cache-Control: no-store (verified by GET, not HEAD), so neither copy can ever be served from cache. :3361 then re-runs it on setInterval(go, 300000). Measured 18,996 bytes per fetch: ~38KB per load plus ~19KB every 5 min (~228KB/h) per open tab, indefinitely. The comment at :3233 claims the re-read is '(cached)' -- it is not. Have the enrichment IIFE consume the already-fetched payload via a resolved promise or a module-scoped cache. Related low: dashboard/data/backfill.json has been byte-identical for 34 days but is force-refetched every load because the cache-bust is applied indiscriminately (:1176), defeating its own max-age=600.
